vix.note: VIX near 17.4 in early Monday trade, up about 3.7 percent from Thursday's 16.78 close as US cash equities reopen after Friday's Juneteenth holiday. It stays subdued in absolute terms and far below the 35.3 one-year high; the swing factors are Fed Chair Warsh's hike-leaning dot plot and fragile US-Iran de-escalation headlines.

macro: Markets reopen after Juneteenth into a hawkish-Fed, lower-oil backdrop. US equities last closed Thursday June 18 (Friday June 19 was the Juneteenth holiday) and reopen Monday June 22 still digesting new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's hawkish first meeting, where the dot plot showed nine of eighteen officials now penciling in a 2026 rate hike. Intel remains the standout, holding above $130 on Apple-foundry speculation that drew a Mizuho price-target raise to $135, while Broadcom's custom-ASIC story gains traction as the AI trade broadens beyond Nvidia. Oil stays on the back foot as fragile US-Iran de-escalation talks cap crude (USO near $115, down from above $140 in early June), and Nvidia edged lower pre-market as investors weigh the geopolitics.
action: Let Monday's reopen confirm direction before adding; favor staged adds in quality AI-infrastructure (NVDA, AVGO, SMH) on weakness over chasing the unconfirmed Intel foundry pop, keep oil underweight, and hold gold despite its pullback as a Fed and geopolitics hedge.

long.h: Intel's Apple-Driven Surge Gets a Harsh Verdict: 'Becoming a Meme Stock'
long.p: Speculation over an Apple foundry partnership keeps fueling Intel's AI-manufacturing optimism even as some analysts warn the rally has run ahead of confirmed terms; Mizuho lifted its target to $135 while keeping a Neutral rating. A genuine anchor foundry customer would still be a structural turnaround catalyst.

long.h: The AI Boom Enters a New Phase, and Nvidia May Not Be the Biggest Winner
long.p: As the AI buildout shifts from training toward inference, Broadcom's custom ASICs are positioned to capture a growing share; AVGO AI semiconductor revenue hit $10.8B, up 143 percent year over year in fiscal Q2. The custom-silicon theme is broadening the trade beyond Nvidia's GPUs.

long.h: Amazon: You Aren't Bullish Enough as Trainium Moves to External Sales
long.p: AWS is moving its custom Trainium AI chips from internal use toward potential external sales, adding to a roughly $225B revenue commitment and accelerating Bedrock and AWS growth. The shift deepens the custom-silicon threat to Nvidia while strengthening Amazon's own margin story.

long.h: Memory Crisis So Severe Even Apple Cannot Be Safe
long.p: Tim Cook told the WSJ that Apple will raise product prices because of the ongoing memory shortage, underscoring how the AI-driven DRAM squeeze is hitting even the largest buyers. Micron and other memory names continue to rally on the same dynamic.

long.h: Should Investors Worry That Fed Chair Warsh Isn't Cutting Rates?
long.p: Elevated inflation is forcing the central bank's hand, and Warsh's no-cut, hike-leaning stance pressures long-duration growth and rate-sensitive names. The shift reframes the setup for megacap tech and the broad market into the second half.

short.h: Nvidia Edges Down Early Monday as Traders Assess US-Iran Talks
short.p: Nvidia was slightly lower in early Monday trade as investors weighed the latest US-Iran peace-talk headlines after the Juneteenth break. The AI-demand thesis stays intact, but geopolitics is the near-term swing factor for high-beta semis.

short.h: Mizuho Raises Intel Price Target to $135 Amid Foundry Frenzy
short.p: Mizuho Securities lifted its Intel target from $128 to $135 on Sunday while reiterating a Neutral rating, reflecting both the foundry optimism and lingering caution on execution. Intel sits at $133.99 after its 10.6 percent Thursday surge.

short.h: Oil Stays Soft as Postponed US-Iran Talks Temper Ceasefire Hopes
short.p: Crude remains capped after a scheduled US-Iran round in Switzerland was postponed, with Brent slipping on mixed signals; USO has fallen from above $140 in early June to about $115. The de-escalation path is fragile but keeps the energy trade on the back foot.

short.h: Musk Lifts Tesla Voting Stake to Nearly 20%; SpaceX Merger Talk Builds
short.p: Elon Musk exercised about 304 million options last week, raising his Tesla voting power to nearly 20 percent, while the record SpaceX IPO gives him public stock as merger currency. The moves keep cross-Musk deal speculation alive around Tesla.

short.h: Intel and Micron Head for Further Records as Memory Squeeze Bites
short.p: Barron's flags Intel and Micron pressing toward fresh records on the AI-driven memory shortage, even as Alphabet and some other big-tech names slip in Monday's tape. Semis breadth remains the market's high-beta engine.

notes.NVDA: Nvidia at $210.69 (June 18 close) and edging lower pre-market Monday as traders weigh US-Iran headlines. Jensen Huang's recent comments point to still-strong hardware demand, though Amazon Trainium and Google TPU competition are a growing structural overhang.
notes.MSFT: Microsoft at $379.40, still well off its early-June 428 level as the post-FOMC rate reset weighs on long-duration megacap tech. Azure and AI demand remain the core driver into the July 29 print.
notes.INTC: Intel at $133.99 after a 10.6 percent surge on Apple-foundry speculation; Mizuho raised its target to $135 (Neutral) and skeptics call the move meme-like. High risk until an anchor foundry customer is formally confirmed.
notes.AVGO: Broadcom at $411.35; the custom-ASIC and AI-networking thesis is gaining traction as the AI trade broadens beyond Nvidia, with AI semi revenue up 143 percent year over year. Still below its early-June 479 print after the AI-spend wobble.
notes.QQQ: Nasdaq-100 ETF at $740.62 heading into Monday's reopen; megacap tech and semis led the prior rebound but rate-hike risk keeps the index two-way into the next Fed signal.
notes.SMH: Semis ETF at $659.88 after a powerful Intel- and Micron-led recovery. The group is the market's high-beta engine and the memory squeeze is a tailwind; volatility cuts both ways.
notes.SPY: S&P 500 ETF at $746.74, just below its early-June highs as improving semis breadth offsets Fed-driven caution. The hawkish dot plot is the main overhang.
notes.AAPL: Apple at $298.01; the reported Intel US-foundry tie-up is a supply-chain positive, but Tim Cook flagged product price hikes as the memory shortage squeezes even Apple. Margin pressure is the near-term watch ahead of the July 30 report.
notes.AMZN: Amazon at $244.39; AWS is reportedly moving Trainium AI chips toward external sales, and bulls see EPS growth above 20 percent over three years. Self-funded capex and cloud strength keep the medium-term setup intact.
notes.META: Meta at $577.22, still well off its $628 peak after an executive-departure report earlier in the month. AI-driven ad targeting is the core driver; sentiment has softened.
notes.GOOGL: Alphabet at $368.03; Google is using its balance sheet to build a rival AI-chip business around its TPUs, reinforcing the silicon-independence story, though it slipped in Monday's early tape. Reports July 22.
notes.TSLA: Tesla at $400.49; Musk lifted his voting stake to nearly 20 percent and SpaceX-merger speculation is building, while a fatal driver-assist crash is a reminder of headline risk. High-risk into the July 22 print.
notes.NFLX: Netflix at $77.38, stabilizing after breaking below its prior 80 to 86 range. Ad tier and live content remain structural positives; near-term momentum is soft.
notes.DIS: Disney at $103.89, a relative outperformer; streaming profitability and Parks resilience give it a defensive feel versus high-beta tech.
notes.BRKB: Berkshire at $489.46, the portfolio's ballast. Record cash and a defensive mix keep it the steady anchor as rate uncertainty rises.
notes.DIA: Dow ETF at $515.52; value and broad-market participation cushion the tech-led swings. Less exposed to the AI-derating debate than QQQ.
notes.OIL: USO at $114.87, down hard from above $140 in early June as fragile US-Iran de-escalation talks and easing supply fears weigh. Underweight stance intact on the geopolitical path.
notes.BTC: Bitcoin near $63,200 (June 21), firmer over the weekend after dipping mid-week, with the hawkish Fed and a stronger dollar still a headwind. Holding the low-60s; high volatility persists.
notes.GOLD: Gold near $4,194 (June 21), easing further as the post-FOMC dollar and real-rate bounce pressure the metal; still a core hedge but well below the early-June 4,505 high.
notes.SILVER: Silver near $65.6 (June 21), soft alongside gold and its industrial-demand leg; higher volatility than gold makes it the more aggressive hedge.
